elements.text: Dawn breaks cold over the jagged peaks of the Summit of Scars, painting the rising smoke in shades of bruised purple and gold. Below, in the narrow gorge, the vanguard of the aggressive sect is funneled directly into the array's silent, waiting perimeter.
The wind... it has stopped. Kaelen, they have not yet crossed the threshold. We can still draw them out. We can meet them with steel. Honor does not demand this silence.
Kaelen does not look up from the central array stone. His fingers, smeared with ritual soot, trace the final boundary line with mechanical, terrifying precision.

Steel is a variable, Lyra. Variables introduce failure. The math of this gorge is absolute. When the seal breaks, the pressure will ensure zero survivors. Including the rearguard we left to bait them.
Those are our people! They trusted us to hold the flank! You promised them a defensive retreat!
There is no retreat from a landslide, child. He told them what they needed to hear to stand their ground. Without their stand, the sect would never have gathered in the throat of the pass.
elements.expression: gravely, his heavy gaze fixed on the valley below

elements.text: Kaelen presses his palm to the center rune. A deep, sub-audible hum vibrates through the solid stone beneath their feet. The valley below suddenly ignites—not with fire, but with a blinding, crushing vacuum of pure spiritual pressure.

The silence that follows is absolute. Far below, the flashing steel of the sect and the banner of the rearguard vanish instantly, collapsed into a sickening, crimson-stained dust that coats the canyon walls. There are no screams, only the terrible, echoing crack of shifting tectonic plates.
Lyra drops her sword. The heavy blade clatters uselessly against the stone. She collapses to her knees, staring into the empty, silent void where an entire army—and their own allies—just existed.
No... this is not a victory. This is a slaughterhouse. We are no better than the monsters we fought. We sold our souls for a graveyard.
elements.expression: whispering, tears tracing clean lines through the ash on her face

elements.text: We bought our survival, Lyra. The cost of an iron heart is always paid in the blood of those who trusted its strength.
elements.expression: hollow, his world-weary voice carrying a weight heavier than the mountain itself

elements.text: Kaelen slowly stands, his hands clean of the soot now, though his face remains utterly impassive. He looks down at the silent, smoking gorge, his analytical mind already mapping the secure borders of their newly won territory, yet his eyes reflect only the cold, dead light of dawn.
The sect is broken. The region is secure. We proceed to the next phase.
